title: Philotheca wonganensis
intro: Philotheca wonganensis is a plant species native to Western Australia, commonly known as Wongan Philotheca. This plant has been traditionally used to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety, with reported health benefits including improved sleep quality and reduced stress levels. The bioactive constituents of Philotheca wonganensis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Philotheca wonganensis include teas, tinctures, and infused oils, often used to support the nervous system and promote a sense of calm.
taxonomy: Philotheca wonganensis is a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is a member of the Philotheca genus and is native to Western Australia. This plant is a part of the citrus family, Rutaceae, and is also known as Waxflower. The classification of Philotheca wonganensis is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Rosids, Order: Sapindales, Family: Rutaceae, Genus: Philotheca, Species: P. wonganensis. Philotheca wonganensis is a relatively rare and unique species within its genus.
